name: DOGE Session Drive Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: opening-range continuation after directional session dominance
thesis: DOGE 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether session-driven directional control can produce a second-leg continuation after the dominant session establishes range leadership. The edge is participation in broad follow-through, not blind breakout chasing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if session-drive continuation stays too sparse, if dominant-session moves fail to produce second-leg follow-through, or if the setup only captures one-bar bursts that mean-revert immediately.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: One session structurally dominates the 4h bar, price holds near session-led value rather than fully mean reverting, and continuation engages on the follow-through leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition regimes often print uneven participation where one session does the real work and the next leg extends that imbalance before mean reversion catches up.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is appropriate because session-led surges are visible and amplitude is high enough to support partial exits plus a runner.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market is actually pure chop, session dominance will not persist long enough and continuation entries will stall.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade with acceptable density but the runner rarely pays, shorten the management structure before changing the continuation trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_trade_density: Low-to-moderate; denser than exact opening-range rituals, but still selective.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Mixed hit rate with a few cleaner continuation bursts carrying expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE plus at least one cross-lane show credible density and post-cost continuation after the session-driven move.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, thin low-trade outputs, or repeated hold entries that fail to produce a second leg.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low; hold and continuation are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept reasonable by broad dominance/hold logic instead of exact intraday windows

validation_basket.why_included: Best fit for session-led momentum with enough amplitude to pay asymmetric exits.
validation_basket.continue_if: Continuation appears with credible density and positive post-cost behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only isolated one-bar spikes appear or density collapses.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A small but tradable set of dominant-session holds should extend into a second leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on a cleaner major lane rather than only meme-asset volatility.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows comparable but cleaner hold-and-follow-through behavior.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller but more orderly continuation than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Stress-tests whether session-drive continuation survives on a wilder structurally different lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO preserves the setup shape without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO shows only chaos or no repeatable session-led continuation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More violent but still recognizable second-leg continuation after dominant-session control.
